description_long: This is a mature head of Savoy cabbage, characterized by its large, deeply crinkled, and overlapping green leaves. The outer leaves are darker and more textured, curling outward and partially enveloping the lighter, more compact inner leaves. Prominent white veins run through each leaf, creating a striking contrast against the green surface. The cabbage head is round and slightly flattened at the base, with a dense, layered structure typical of this vegetable. The surface appears semi-matte with a natural, slightly waxy sheen, and the leaf edges are irregular and wavy. No central stem is visible, and some outer leaves are loosely attached, giving the cabbage a slightly open appearance.
description: A round, crinkled Savoy cabbage with dark green, wavy outer leaves and lighter, compact inner layers. Prominent white veins and a slightly open structure are visible.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cabbage's open, wavy outer leaves and the lighter, compact inner leaves with pronounced veining.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curling of the outer leaves and the dense layering of the inner head, with strong leaf texture.
description_view_2: Another angle showing the cabbage's rounded form, with a clear view of the overlapping, crinkled leaves and a small gap at the base.
description_view_3: Back view displays the most intact, continuous surface of the cabbage, with the outer leaves closely hugging the head and less visible inner structure.
